commands.review_summary:
Adds a 5 MiB RSS podcast transcript response cap in core and new Vitest coverage for oversized Content-Length, oversized streamed, and small transcript responses.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence current-main path: an RSS `<podcast:transcript>` response reaches `tryFetchTranscriptFromFeedXml` and is fully materialized via `res.text()`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-visible proof and a small code repair, so it should stay in human review rather than an automated repair lane while the proof gate is mock-only.

target.title: [security] fix(core): cap RSS transcript response size
target.body:
## Summary

This PR hardens RSS `<podcast:transcript>` fetching so feed-controlled transcript URLs cannot force the process to materialize arbitrarily large response bodies in memory.

- Adds a bounded reader for RSS transcript responses.
- Rejects oversized `Content-Length` values before reading the body.
- Stops streaming responses once the byte cap is exceeded.
- Keeps the existing network/SSRF guard and transcript parsing behavior intact for normal small transcripts.
- Adds focused regression coverage for known-length oversized, unknown-length oversized, and benign small transcript responses.

## Security issues covered

| Issue | Impact | Severity |
|-------|--------|----------|
| Unbounded RSS transcript body materialization | A feed-controlled `<podcast:transcript>` URL can return a very large response that is fully read via `res.text()`, creating memory/CPU exhaustion risk during podcast summarization. | Medium |

## Before this PR

- RSS `<podcast:transcript>` URLs were fetched through the existing network guard, but the response body had no byte limit.
- The transcript body was read with `res.text()`, so a large attacker-controlled response could be fully materialized before parsing.
- Existing tests covered SSRF/network-guard behavior, but not transcript response-size limits.

## After this PR

- RSS transcript responses are capped at 5 MiB.
- Oversized `Content-Length` responses are rejected before body reads.
- Streaming responses without a known length are rejected once they exceed the cap.
- Small `text/plain`, VTT, and JSON transcript responses still flow through the existing parser path.
- Regression tests now lock in the size-limit behavior alongside the existing SSRF tests.

## Why this matters

Podcast feeds and feed metadata can be supplied from outside the local process. Even when URL resolution is guarded against SSRF, the response body still needs an explicit resource boundary. Without a byte cap, a feed can point to a very large transcript body and force the summarizer to spend memory and CPU before the transcript parser can decide whether the content is useful.

## How this differs from related issue/PR

This is related to the same resource-boundary family as #285, but it covers a different fetch path and sink:

- #285 capped remote asset extraction output in `src/run/flows/asset/extract.ts`.
- This PR caps RSS podcast transcript responses in `packages/core/src/content/transcript/providers/podcast/rss-transcript.ts`.
- The trigger here is feed metadata via `<podcast:transcript>`, not the asset-extraction flow.
- Both fixes are needed because each path fetches and materializes untrusted remote content independently.

## Attack flow

```text
Attacker-controlled or untrusted podcast/RSS feed
    -> <podcast:transcript url="https://example.test/huge.txt">
        -> RSS transcript fetch passes URL/network guard
            -> response body is read without a byte cap
                -> process memory/CPU can be exhausted before parsing completes
```

## Affected code

| Issue | Files |
|-------|-------|
| Unbounded RSS transcript body materialization | `packages/core/src/content/transcript/providers/podcast/rss-transcript.ts`, `tests/security.rss-transcript-size-limit.test.ts` |

## Root cause

Issue: Unbounded RSS transcript body materialization

- The URL fetch path had SSRF-oriented network guarding, but no response-size boundary.
- The code used `await res.text()` directly, which reads the entire response body before parsing or rejecting it.
- Existing regression coverage did not exercise oversized transcript responses.

## CVSS assessment

| Issue | CVSS v3.1 | Vector |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Unbounded RSS transcript body materialization | 5.3 Medium | `CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L` |

Rationale:

- The impact is availability-focused: a malicious or compromised feed can force unnecessary memory/CPU consumption during summarization.
- User interaction is required in the typical workflow because a user or automation must summarize the attacker-controlled feed/podcast item.
- The patch does not claim data disclosure or code execution.

## Safe reproduction steps

1. Use a feed item containing a `<podcast:transcript>` URL controlled by the test.
2. Serve or mock a transcript response larger than the intended resource boundary.
3. On vulnerable code, observe that the transcript body is accepted/materialized without a `Range` request or response-size rejection.
4. With this PR, run the focused regression tests and observe that oversized known-length and streamed responses are rejected while a small transcript is accepted.

Docker verification was used for both sides of the proof:

- Before fix: a focused PoC in `node:24-bookworm` reproduced that an 8 MiB RSS transcript body was accepted/materialized by the vulnerable path.
- After fix: the focused regression tests and existing RSS transcript SSRF tests pass in `node:24-bookworm`.

## Expected vulnerable behavior

- A feed-supplied RSS transcript URL can return a large body.
- The vulnerable code reads the entire response with `res.text()`.
- The safe proof signal is a bounded mocked/served transcript body being fully accepted before the patch, then rejected after this PR once it exceeds the configured cap.

## Changes in this PR

- Adds `MAX_RSS_TRANSCRIPT_BYTES` for RSS podcast transcript responses.
- Replaces direct `res.text()` usage with `readTranscriptTextWithLimit()`.
- Rejects oversized `Content-Length` before consuming the body.
- Enforces the same byte cap while reading streamed responses.
- Preserves existing network guard and redirect handling.
- Adds regression tests for oversized `Content-Length`, streaming over-limit responses, and small accepted transcripts.

## Files changed

| Category | Files | What changed |
|----------|-------|--------------|
| Core hardening | `packages/core/src/content/transcript/providers/podcast/rss-transcript.ts` | Adds a byte-capped transcript reader and uses it for RSS transcript fetches. |
| Regression tests | `tests/security.rss-transcript-size-limit.test.ts` | Covers known-length oversized, streamed oversized, and small accepted transcript responses. |

## Maintainer impact

- The change is limited to RSS podcast transcript fetching.
- Existing SSRF/network-guard behavior remains in place.
- Normal small transcripts continue to be accepted and parsed.
- Very large RSS transcript responses are now skipped rather than fully materialized.
- No CLI, daemon, extension, release, or dependency changes are included.

## Fix rationale

The RSS transcript fetch path already treats the URL as untrusted enough to use a network guard. A response-size boundary belongs at the same trust boundary so the process does not need to fully allocate attacker-controlled response bodies before parsing. Checking `Content-Length` handles simple oversized responses early, while streaming enforcement covers unknown-length and misleading-length responses.

## Type of change

- [x] Security fix
- [x] Tests
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor with no behavior change

## Test plan

- [x] Docker before-fix proof: focused PoC reproduced vulnerable 8 MiB RSS transcript materialization in `node:24-bookworm`.
- [x] Docker after-fix validation:

```bash
docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$PWD":/work -w /work node:24-bookworm bash -lc 'corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10.33.2 --activate && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts && pnpm -s build && pnpm -s typecheck && pnpm -s format:check && pnpm -s lint && pnpm -s vitest run tests/security.rss-transcript-size-limit.test.ts tests/security.rss-transcript-ssrf.test.ts && git diff --check'
```

Result:

```text
✓ tests/security.rss-transcript-size-limit.test.ts (3 tests)
✓ tests/security.rss-transcript-ssrf.test.ts (13 tests)

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       16 passed (16)
```

Also passed in the same Docker run:

- `pnpm -s build`
- `pnpm -s typecheck`
- `pnpm -s format:check`
- `pnpm -s lint`
- `git diff --check`

## Disclosure notes

- This PR is intentionally scoped to RSS podcast transcript response-size limits.
- It does not claim code execution, data disclosure, or a bypass of the existing SSRF/network guard.
- It treats #285 as same-family prior resource-boundary hardening, not as an exact duplicate of this transcript-fetch path.
- No unrelated files are changed.
target.author: hinotoi-agent
